---
title: "Fishbone Diagram: Poisoning in South Africa"
format: html
editor: source
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

*Keyword labels on the diagram are cross-referenced to the table below for full descriptions.*

```{r}
#| label: fishbone-diagram
#| fig-width: 22
#| fig-height: 13
#| out-width: "100%"
#| dpi: 150

# install.packages("SixSigma", repos = "http://cran.us.r-project.org")
library(SixSigma)

# Short codes shown on the diagram — format: [category letter][number]
ss.ceDiag(
  effect = "Poisoning in\nSouth Africa",
  causes.gr = c(
    "A — Law & Bans",
    "B — Enforcement",
    "C — Occupational",
    "D — Community",
    "E — Structural",
    "F — Surveillance",
    "G — Coordination",
    "H — Mental Health",
    "I — Clinical"
  ),
  causes = list(
    # A — Law & Bans
    c("Pesticide phase-out", "Legacy registrations", "WHO alignment",
      "Ingredient review", "Upstream bans"),
    # B — Enforcement
    c("Regulation enforcement", "Market access", "Informal inspection",
      "Repackaging control", "Too few EHPs", "Seizure authority"),
    # C — Occupational
    c("Worker protections", "Handling monitoring", "PPE access",
      "Exposure despite training", "On-farm storage"),
    # D — Community
    c("Parent awareness", "Shop owner awareness", "Home storage",
      "Unlabelled packaging", "Misleading containers", "Safer alternatives"),
    # E — Structural
    c("Waste management", "Poverty & crowding", "Rural governance",
      "Pesticide dependence", "Limited alternatives"),
    # F — Surveillance
    c("Burden unknown", "Exposure tracking", "Notification detail",
      "Agent profiling", "Death intent data", "Data linkage"),
    # G — Coordination
    c("Inter-dept coordination", "Notification → investigation",
      "Investigation → action", "Response delays", "Accountability loops"),
    # H — Mental Health
    c("MH service access", "Self-harm pathways", "GBV & conflict",
      "Psychosocial distress", "Post-harm follow-up"),
    # I — Clinical
    c("Recognition delays", "Treatment pathways", "Poison information",
      "Referral systems", "Emergency–MH integration")
  ),
  main = "Cause-and-Effect Diagram",
  sub  = "Data-to-policy framing for poisoning prevention in South Africa — see table below for full descriptions"
)
```

```{r}
#| label: reference-table

library(dplyr)
library(knitr)

ref <- data.frame(
  Code = c(
    # A
    "Pesticide phase-out", "Legacy registrations", "WHO alignment",
    "Ingredient review", "Upstream bans",
    # B
    "Regulation enforcement", "Market access", "Informal inspection",
    "Repackaging control", "Too few EHPs", "Seizure authority",
    # C
    "Worker protections", "Handling monitoring", "PPE access",
    "Exposure despite training", "On-farm storage",
    # D
    "Parent awareness", "Shop owner awareness", "Home storage",
    "Unlabelled packaging", "Misleading containers", "Safer alternatives",
    # E
    "Waste management", "Poverty & crowding", "Rural governance",
    "Pesticide dependence", "Limited alternatives",
    # F
    "Burden unknown", "Exposure tracking", "Notification detail",
    "Agent profiling", "Death intent data", "Data linkage",
    # G
    "Inter-dept coordination", "Notification → investigation",
    "Investigation → action", "Response delays", "Accountability loops",
    # H
    "MH service access", "Self-harm pathways", "GBV & conflict",
    "Psychosocial distress", "Post-harm follow-up",
    # I
    "Recognition delays", "Treatment pathways", "Poison information",
    "Referral systems", "Emergency–MH integration"
  ),
  Category = c(
    rep("A — Law, Bans & Registration Review", 5),
    rep("B — Regulation, Enforcement & Supply Chains", 6),
    rep("C — Occupational Protection", 5),
    rep("D — Community, Household Exposure & Packaging", 6),
    rep("E — Structural & Environmental Drivers", 5),
    rep("F — Surveillance, Death Data & Toxicology", 6),
    rep("G — Coordination & Closed-Loop Response", 5),
    rep("H — Mental Health, GBV & Psychosocial Drivers", 5),
    rep("I — Clinical Readiness & Poison Information", 5)
  ),
  Description = c(
    # A
    "Slow phase-out of highly hazardous pesticides",
    "Legacy registrations remain in active use",
    "Incomplete alignment with WHO hazard classifications",
    "Weak routine review of high-risk active ingredients",
    "Underuse of upstream bans and restrictions",
    # B
    "Weak enforcement of pesticide regulations",
    "Restricted products remain accessible in informal markets",
    "Weak inspection of informal trade and retail",
    "Poor control of repackaging into unlabelled containers",
    "Too few inspectors and Environmental Health Practitioners",
    "Limited legal authority to seize or remove products",
    # C
    "Weak implementation of farmworker protection legislation",
    "Limited monitoring of agricultural chemical handling",
    "PPE access and affordability barriers",
    "Continued occupational exposure despite awareness campaigns",
    "Unsafe on-farm storage and handling practices",
    # D
    "Low poisoning awareness among parents and communities",
    "Low awareness among informal shop owners and traders",
    "Unsafe household storage of pesticides and chemicals",
    "Informal packaging sold without hazard warnings",
    "Toxic products stored or sold in misleading containers",
    "Limited knowledge of safer pest-control alternatives",
    # E
    "Poor waste management leading to pest infestations",
    "Poverty and overcrowding increasing exposure risk",
    "Weak local and rural governance of chemical use",
    "Community dependence on hazardous pesticides",
    "Limited availability of practical safer alternatives",
    # F
    "Poor understanding of the true poisoning burden",
    "Weak real-time exposure and incident surveillance",
    "Insufficient detail captured in case notifications",
    "Limited toxicological agent profiling in non-fatal cases",
    "Poor manner-of-death and intent detail in mortality data",
    "Weak data linkage across health, forensic and administrative systems",
    # G
    "Weak interdepartmental coordination on chemical safety",
    "Case notification not systematically linked to field investigation",
    "Investigation findings not linked to regulatory or policy action",
    "Delays between complaint receipt and field response",
    "Weak accountability mechanisms and feedback loops",
    # H
    "Poor community access to mental health support services",
    "Inadequate crisis pathways for self-poisoning presentations",
    "GBV and family conflict as drivers of intentional poisoning",
    "Underlying psychosocial distress and hopelessness",
    "Poor clinical and social follow-up after self-harm events",
    # I
    "Delayed clinical recognition of poisoning presentations",
    "Variable and inconsistent treatment pathways across facilities",
    "Poor access to poison information by clinicians (no 24 h line)",
    "Weak inter-facility referral systems for severe cases",
    "Limited integration of emergency and mental-health services"
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

knitr::kable(
  ref,
  col.names = c("Diagram Label", "Category", "Full Description"),
  caption   = "Table 1. Reference descriptions for fishbone diagram labels",
  align     = c("c", "l", "l")
)
```
